confirmed.title: ReleaseNode deletes ◆ Built node claims with no immutability guard (§3.1.1)
confirmed.detail: The ReleaseNode arm (commands.rs:1571-1585) removes the claim from state and from factory.node_claims WITHOUT calling require_planned — it is the only delete command that skips the guard (DeleteFactory/Group/Port/Edge/Junction/Route/Switch all call it). DECISIONS.md:70 explicitly deferred this: "ReleaseNode unguarded is an acknowledged deferral UNTIL import creates ◆ claims." That precondition is now met: W2b-C import creates Status::Built NodeClaims (crates/app/src/import.rs:702), and Session::edit (session.rs:460) special-cases only SetBuildDone, so a release_node command reaches commands::apply unguarded. Failure scenario: import a save containing a miner → state.node_claims holds a claim C with status="built", created_by=Import. Fire {type:"release_node",id:C} (map drawer release, or /api/edit) → returns Ok and deletes C; no BuiltImmutable error. This silently drops game-ground-truth from the plan, inconsistent with SetClaim which was carefully written to flip Built→Planned (converting, never deleting) rather than mutate the ◆ baseline. Correct behavior per §3.1.1 is to reject (or convert), matching DeleteGroup on a built group.

confirmed.detail: Session::edit (session.rs:460-518) commits every applied transaction through commit_mutation → UndoLog::push with no guard for an empty forward batch, and UndoLog::push (undo.rs:77-81) unconditionally truncates the redo tail and advances the cursor. confirmed.title: T0 drag preview collapses an independent output chain to 0 when ANY sibling target is currently infeasible
confirmed.detail: In t0::solve the ceiling analysis (lines ~347-358) probes the edited port at t=0 while holding ALL sibling output targets fixed at their snapshot rates. If those fixed siblings alone violate any belt/ceiling (min_slack(f0) < -REL_TOL), it sets base_feasible=false and returns ceiling=(0.0, worst0) — the most-violated SIBLING constraint. Then the clamp at lines ~420-425 fires unconditionally: targets[port] (>0) > max_rate(0) → the EDITED port is clamped to 0 and target_ceiling names the sibling's constraint. Concrete repro: one factory, two independent chains — in-a(open)->ga->out-x and in-b(ceiling 10)->gb->out-y with out-y.rate=30 (infeasible, 30>10). User drags out-x's slider up. buildSnapshot includes out-y at 30; the T0 base probe sees in-b over its ceiling, so out-x is clamped to 0 with binding=InputCeiling(in-b). The optimistic preview (projected.result) shows out-x's port and its whole chain at 0/min while the user drags it UP, and misattributes out-x's 'ceiling' to the unrelated in-b port. T1 (used on release, ceiling pass keeps siblings elastic) computes out-x's true ceiling, so the number snaps to correct on release — this is a T0-only preview bug and a T0/T1 divergence. Trigger is common: any capped output whose target currently exceeds its cap (set via wizard/proposal/import or a lowered ceiling after a claim change).

CONFIRMED — reproduced exactly against the live solver crate. The finding is real.

confirmed.title: Deficit `needed` for a multi-output factory is scaled by the WRONG output's target → phantom / inflated deficits
confirmed.detail: In empire_solve's deficit block (~line 2148) `requested` is taken from the FIRST out port of the starved factory: `f.ports.iter().find_map(|pid| (p.direction==Out).then_some(p.rate))`. It is then used both to GATE the deficit (`starved_by_ceiling = requested > max_rate`) and to SIZE it (`needed = flow * requested / max_rate`, ~line 2177). `max_rate` is the InputCeiling ceiling for the specific route-fed input port `dst_port`, i.e. the achievable rate of the OUTPUT the edit targeted. For a factory with more than one Out port these are two different outputs. Concrete: factory F has Out port A (item X, rate 100, created first) and Out port B (item Y, rate 20, fed by a route-capped input inY whose belt supplies only 5/min). User edits SetTarget on B → t1 reports target_ceiling{binding: InputCeiling(inY), max_rate:5}. Session computes requested=A.rate=100, so needed = flow*100/5 (≈100 when flow=5) instead of B's true requirement (~20 for a 1:1 recipe) — a 5× inflation. Worse, `starved_by_ceiling = 100 > 5` fires even when B's real target (say 3) is fully met, registering a PHANTOM deficit for a factory that is not starved. These deficit rows feed deficit_repair/route_bottleneck_fix (opportunities.rs), the advisor new_deficit card, and chat — so the NEXT-MOVES 'short N/min' figure and the wizard prefill rate inherit the inflated/phantom number. Differential proof: with B held fixed, raising A's target from 100→200 doubles `needed` for inY, though B's requirement is independent of A. All existing e2e specs use single-output factories, so this is unpinned.

confirmed.detail: apply_sync's SyncOp::UpdateGroup demolished arm (count==0, import.rs ~L1255-1263) removes the MachineGroup and retains it from factory.groups, but — unlike the DeleteGroup command (commands.rs L1085-1112) and remove_factory_cascading (L713) — it never removes the belt edges touching that group nor the boundary ports that were fed only by it. confirmed.detail: surplus() computes nameplate potential as `perMachine * effCount(g) * Math.max(effClock(g), 1)` (line 80). effClock returns the PLAN clock as a fraction (1 = 100%), not the solved clock, so it is never 0 — the `Math.max(_, 1)` never helps the idle case it claims to defend and instead inflates any machine the user set below 100%. confirmed.title: Group↔boundary-port belts are mislabeled as cross-floor lifts (ports have no floor)
confirmed.detail: In the edges memo, floorOfEnd() returns 0 for any port end (line 562-567) and `lift = srcFloor !== dstFloor` (line 570). Ports have no `floor` field (state/types.ts Port), so any group/junction placed on a non-zero floor that connects to a boundary port is treated as a lift. This is the common state after AUTO-FLOOR (final-assembly groups get high floors, their OUT-port belts descend to floor-0 ports). Failure scenario A (all-floors view): a group G on floor 2 exporting to OUT port P renders the G→P belt with a bogus '⇅ F2→F0' lift tag and diamond lift pads (BeltEdgeView lines 99, 138-141, 196) even though P is a floor-agnostic boundary. Failure scenario B (floor filter): filter to F2 — G is shown, and P is shown (ports are never floor-dimmed, GraphView lines 409-421), but the G→P belt is replaced by a phantom lift-portal stub 72px right of G pointing to 'F0' (lines 576-601); the actual belt to the still-visible port P disappears, and clicking the portal jumps to floor 0 where G is now hidden. Expected: a group/junction→port edge is not a lift; it should draw normally and never portal, since the port is present on every floor.

CONFIRMED as a genuine defect. I traced the code end-to-end and could not refute it.

confirmed.title: Wrong-but-valid JSON-array uploaded as an empty "uploaded" catalog (false success, plan stranded)
confirmed.detail: parse_docs only errors when the top level is not a JSON array (Shape) or the bytes are not JSON (Json). A JSON file whose top level IS an array but contains no recognizable Docs sections — e.g. `[]`, or some other tool's array-shaped export — parses to Ok(GameData) with 0 items/recipes/machines; there is no non-emptiness check anywhere on the upload path. WebSession::new therefore succeeds, buildVersion flips "fixture"→"uploaded", and store.uploadDocs fires a GREEN success toast `Catalog loaded — 0 recipes`. Concrete failure: user picks the wrong .json (an array-shaped file). Observable result: (1) the onboarding gate `catalogLoaded = buildVersion!=="fixture"` turns OFF, hiding the '① Upload Docs.json' step so they believe a catalog is loaded; (2) any save they then import matches 0 recipes; (3) any pre-existing plan's groups now reference recipes absent from the catalog. Truncated bytes and object-shaped JSON both DO produce a friendly error, so this array case is the one class that slips through as a false success. Fix: reject a catalog with 0 recipes (or 0 machines) at upload with a friendly error rather than accepting it.

probes.drive: Via bridge /api/edit seed three self-contained subjects (unique names/coords): (1) OVERDRAWN grid — GEN1 with __PowerMW OUT set to 30, LOAD1 with 16x Build_SmelterMk1_C Recipe_IngotIron_C (64 MW draw), add_route {kind:{kind:power}} GEN1→LOAD1. (2) THIN grid — GEN2 __PowerMW OUT set to 75, LOAD2 16x Build_SmelterMk1_C (64 MW), power route GEN2→LOAD2 (→ ~14% headroom, warn band). (3) STARVED copper chain — producer OUT Desc_CopperIngot_C dipped so a Desc_CopperIngot_C deficit exists (mirror w3/w5 seeding). GET /api/next (default prefs). Then POST /api/next/preferences {"noTrains":false,"ignorePower":true}; GET /api/next again. Restore {"noTrains":false,"ignorePower":false} after.
probes.expected: BEFORE toggle: opportunities contains a kind==='power_deficit' card, a kind==='power_margin' card, and a deficit_repair:Desc_CopperIngot_C card; the power_deficit index < the deficit_repair index (class 0 above class 1). AFTER ignorePower=true: NO kind==='power_margin' card remains; the power_deficit card still exists and its `evidence` ends with the exact suffix ' — power ignored by preference — this grid is still overdrawn'; and the deficit_repair:Desc_CopperIngot_C index is now < the power_deficit index (demotion to class 3, below class-1 repairs).

probes.name: route_bottleneck_fix names the SMALLEST sufficient belt tier for a transport-capped starve
probes.drive: Via bridge /api/edit: PROD factory — IN port Desc_OreCopper_C rateCeiling=480, OUT port Desc_CopperIngot_C rateCeiling=null, group 8x Build_SmelterMk1_C Recipe_IngotCopper_C clock 1.0, belts port→group→port; set_port_rate OUT=100 (producer achieves 100/min; upstream solves blind to the belt). SINK factory — IN port Desc_CopperIngot_C rateCeiling=null, OUT port Desc_Wire_C rateCeiling=null, group 16x Build_ConstructorMk1_C Recipe_Wire_C, belts; set_port_rate wire OUT=200 (→ 100/min copper-ingot demand). add_route {kind:{kind:belt,tier:1}} PROD.OUT→SINK.IN (Mk.1 cap = 60). GET /api/next.
probes.expected: opportunities contains a kind==='route_bottleneck_fix' card for this route whose `title` contains 'bump it to Mk.2' (smallest belt tier with capacity>=flow+recoverable=60+40=100 is Mk.2 at 120) and whose `evidence` is exactly '60.0/60.0 per min at 100% with 40.0/min recoverable through it'. There is NO deficit_repair:Desc_CopperIngot_C card (production_gap is 0 — the producer makes the full 100).

probes.name: deficit_repair names an already-full contributing route when the miss is production, not transport
probes.drive: Via bridge /api/edit: PROD factory — IN Desc_OreIron_C rateCeiling=480, OUT Desc_IronIngot_C rateCeiling=null, group 8x Build_SmelterMk1_C Recipe_IngotIron_C, belts; set_port_rate OUT=60 (producer makes exactly the Mk.1 belt cap). SINK factory — IN Desc_IronIngot_C rateCeiling=null, OUT Desc_IronRod_C rateCeiling=null, group 16x Build_ConstructorMk1_C Recipe_IronRod_C, belts; set_port_rate rod OUT=100 (→ 100/min iron-ingot demand). add_route {kind:{kind:belt,tier:1}} PROD.OUT→SINK.IN. GET /api/next.
probes.expected: opportunities contains deficit_repair:Desc_IronIngot_C with action.kind==='wizardGoal', action.rate===40, and `evidence` exactly 'need 100.0/min, supplied 60.0/min across 1 port(s); the Mk.1 route is already full — upgrading it is also required once production rises'. There is NO route_bottleneck_fix card for this route (transport_gap is 0 → recoverable 0 → the route-fix family is correctly gated out).

probes.name: Two-click wipe DISARMS when the DATA menu closes and reopens
probes.drive: 1) POST http://localhost:8791/api/edit with [{"type":"create_factory","name":"DISARM TEST","position":{"x":-2400,"y":2400},"region":"GRASS FIELDS"}]. 2) page.goto('/'); if getByTestId('onboard-skip') visible, click it; await getByTestId('map-root'). 3) Record N = Object.keys((GET /api/hydrate).plan.factories).length (>=1). 4) Click getByTestId('btn-data-menu'); click getByTestId('btn-new-empire') — assert it now contains /Click again/i (armed). 5) page.keyboard.press('Escape') to close the menu. 6) Click getByTestId('btn-data-menu') to reopen. 7) Click getByTestId('btn-new-empire') exactly ONCE.
probes.expected: After reopen, btn-new-empire reads 'Start new empire' (NOT 'Click again') — the arm was reset by the close. The single post-reopen click only RE-ARMS (button now shows 'Click again to delete everything'); GET /api/hydrate still reports N factories — the plan is NOT wiped. (new-empire.spec pins arm→confirm→wipe but never the disarm-on-close, so a regression that let the arm survive a close would silently make a single click destructive and go uncaught.)

probes.name: Escape while the DATA menu is open closes the menu WITHOUT clearing the map selection
probes.drive: 1) page.goto('/'); await getByTestId('map-root'); dismiss onboarding if present. 2) Select something on the map: locator('.searchbox input').fill('iron ingot works 1'); page.keyboard.press('Enter'); await getByTestId('summary-drawer') visible. 3) Click getByTestId('btn-data-menu'); await getByTestId('data-menu') visible. 4) page.keyboard.press('Escape').
probes.expected: getByTestId('data-menu') becomes hidden AND getByTestId('summary-drawer') is STILL visible — the DataMenu capture-phase handler consumed Escape (stopPropagation) so MapView's window bubble handler never ran setSelection(null). A regression removing the stopPropagation would close the menu and ALSO clear the selection on the same keystroke.

probes.name: ⌘K/Ctrl+K while the DATA menu is open closes the menu and focuses the header search
probes.drive: 1) page.goto('/'); await getByTestId('map-root'); dismiss onboarding if present. 2) Click getByTestId('btn-data-menu'); await getByTestId('data-menu') visible. 3) page.keyboard.press('Control+k').
probes.expected: getByTestId('data-menu') becomes hidden AND locator('.searchbox input') is focused (toBeFocused). The menu's fixed backdrop did not swallow the shortcut: the capture handler closed the menu WITHOUT consuming the key, and SearchBox's bubble handler then focused the search. A regression that consumed ⌘K in the menu (or left the backdrop up) would leave the search unfocused or the menu open.

probes.name: PWR chip shows the generation segment only when generation > 0 (draw-vs-generation truth)
probes.drive: 1) Wipe to a clean plan: POST /api/edit two create_factory cmds, open DATA menu, double-click btn-new-empire to run new_empire (or POST the equivalent reset) so the plan is empty. 2) page.goto('/'); read getByTestId('sb-power'). 3) Assert locator('[data-testid="sb-power"] .sb-gen') has count 0 and the text matches /PWR .*MW/ with no '/'. 4) Create a generator: from GET /api/hydrate gamedata.machines pick a machine whose kind is Generator; POST /api/edit [{create_factory...},{create_group with that machine, count>=1, clock 1.0}]; page.reload(). 5) Read getByTestId('sb-power') again.
probes.expected: With zero generation, sb-power shows draw only — no '.sb-gen' span, no '/ <gen> MW'. After a generator group exists (totalGenerationMw>0), the '.sb-gen' span appears and sb-power text contains ' / ' followed by the generation figure in MW. This pins that the generation half of the chip tracks totalGenerationMw>0 rather than always rendering; no existing spec asserts sb-power content.

probes.name: Garbage/truncated Docs.json upload → friendly error, no wedged state
probes.drive: Web build (playwright.web.config, __ficsitStore/__ficsitBackend handles as in e2e-web/web-smoke.spec.ts). goto('/'); waitReady. dispatch([{type:'create_factory',name:'KEEP',position:{x:1,y:2,z:0},region:'GRASS FIELDS'}]). Assert factoryCount===1 and buildVersion==='fixture'. Write a temp file containing the truncated bytes `{"NativeClass":` (invalid JSON) and page.getByTestId('docs-file-input').setInputFiles(that file).
probes.expected: buildVersion STAYS 'fixture' (never flips to 'uploaded'); a red error toast is shown whose text starts "Couldn't load Docs.json —" and cmdError is non-null; factoryCount stays 1 and factory 'KEEP' is still present; a subsequent dispatch(create_factory 'AFTER') succeeds (app is not wedged); after page.reload()+waitReady, factoryCount===1, factory 'KEEP' present, buildVersion==='fixture'.

probes.name: Wrong-but-valid JSON array (`[]`) Docs.json upload → should be rejected
probes.drive: goto('/'); waitReady. Assert buildVersion==='fixture'. Write a temp file whose entire contents are `[]` and setInputFiles('docs-file-input', that file). Wait up to 30s for the upload to settle.
probes.expected: DECLARED-EXPECTED (correct behavior): the upload is REJECTED — buildVersion remains 'fixture', an error toast appears, and catalogLoaded stays false (the '① Upload Docs.json' onboarding/DATA step is still shown). [This probe pins finding #1: the CURRENT implementation instead flips buildVersion→'uploaded', shows a green 'Catalog loaded — 0 recipes' toast, and turns the first-run gate off — a mismatch that confirms the missing non-emptiness validation.]

probes.name: Docs.json upload preserves an existing plan through the catalog swap and reload
probes.drive: goto('/'); waitReady. dispatch([{type:'create_factory',name:'SURVIVOR',position:{x:3,y:4,z:0},region:'GRASS FIELDS'}]). Assert factoryCount===1, buildVersion==='fixture'. setInputFiles('docs-file-input', crates/gamedata/assets/docs-fixture.json). poll buildVersion until 'uploaded'.
probes.expected: Immediately after the swap: factoryCount===1 and the factory named 'SURVIVOR' is still present (the worker rebuilt WebSession over the new catalog from the preserved plan snapshot). After page.reload()+waitReady: buildVersion==='uploaded' AND factoryCount===1 with 'SURVIVOR' present (saveDocsAndPlan wrote docs+plan atomically). The existing 'corrupt plan keeps docs' test corrupts the plan away, so no current test asserts a GOOD plan survives a GOOD upload.

probes.name: wasm undo snapshot persists the undone state across reload
probes.drive: goto('/'); waitReady. dispatch([{type:'create_factory',name:'ALPHA',...}]) then dispatch([{type:'create_factory',name:'BETA',...}]). Assert factoryCount===2. Call await __ficsitStore.getState().undo() (undoes BETA's creation). Assert factoryCount===1 and the sole factory is 'ALPHA'. page.reload(); waitReady.
probes.expected: After reload factoryCount===1 and the only factory is 'ALPHA' — BETA does NOT resurrect. This pins that the dispatch 'undo' arm's mutated=true snapshot actually wrote the post-undo store to IndexedDB (the undo/redo persistence path is unpinned by web-smoke).

probes.name: View-state (openFactory) round-trips through IndexedDB on reload
probes.drive: goto('/'); waitReady. dispatch([{type:'create_factory',name:'HOME',...}],{select:true}); capture its id F. Call __ficsitStore.getState().setView({mode:'factory',factoryId:F}) (persists openFactory via set_view_state, debounced). Then dispatch([{type:'create_factory',name:'FLUSH',...}]) to force an inline snapshot that flushes the pending debounced view write. page.reload(); waitReady.
probes.expected: After reload, __ficsitStore.getState().view deep-equals {mode:'factory', factoryId:F} — the app reopens the factory it was in (openFactory persisted and hydrate re-projected it), NOT {mode:'map'}. Pins view-state hydrate fidelity and that a real mutation flushes the coalesced view-state snapshot.
